title: Non-record: Deep Supervision on M4 MacBook
author: ranausmanai

data.description: Hourglass/U-Net-style encoder-decoder architecture with skip connections.
Adds an auxiliary next-token prediction loss at the encoder-decoder boundary using the existing final norm and output projection.
Uses RoPE on only part of the head dimensions.

novel_contributions: Introduces deep supervision at the encoder-decoder boundary for this competition.
Finds an optimal deep supervision weight of 0.03 with an inverted-U response.
Shows deep supervision helps at small batch sizes but becomes neutral or slightly harmful at larger batch sizes.
Demonstrates batch scaling improvements up to 128K tokens/step with no plateau observed.
Separates step-count-dependent techniques like EMA/SWA from architecture-dependent techniques like deep supervision.
Reports that EMA, SWA, Partial RoPE + LN Scale, and longer sequence length hurt at 300 steps on the M4 setup.
